change_comments.message:
The second run unfortunately [doesn't look much better](https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/141289-2/). The [cds-blueprints-processor](https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/141289-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/k8s-teardown/pod-onap-cds-blueprints-processor-7cfc766c77-fkzz7-cds-blueprints-processor-logs.html) and the [cds-command-executor](https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/141289-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/k8s-teardown/pod-onap-cds-command-executor-669b6b8cb7-9cbvr-cds-command-executor-logs.html) are both in a crash loop. Perhaps they both have the same issue (because they may use the same libraries?)

The blueprints-processor fails with:

```
2025-07-08 15:47:22,148|||main||||ERROR||||||| Application run failed
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'primaryEntityManager' defined in class path resource [org/onap/ccsdk/cds/blueprintsprocessor/BlueprintDatabaseConfiguration.class]: Invocation of init method failed; nested exception is java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: net/bytebuddy/NamingStrategy$SuffixingRandom$BaseNameResolver

	at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.ClassLoaders$AppClassLoader.loadClass(Unknown Source)

```

[This stackoverflow answer](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/51384547/facing-hibernate-error-caused-by-java-lang-classnotfoundexception-net-bytebud#answer-74710102) suggests to check dependency scope of the `byte-buddy` dependency to resolve the issue.
I see that you indeed changed the scope in [Update to use released parent for Paris](https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/cds/+/140948)

I added that explicit dependency to fix a compile-time error in our unit tests, but didn't realize hibernate also used that same third party library at run time.

Updated to remove the "test" scope, rebuilt CDS dockers, and bumped version to use the new version.  Hopefully that should fix the issue.

The [run for patcheset 3](https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/141289-3/) was better now.
Only `neng` [still has issues coming up](https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/141289-3/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/k8s-teardown/pod-onap-network-name-gen-cb7cd7d45-24c5d-network-name-gen.log) due to running in a java 11 base image.

I gave it a closer look and basically the situation for all services in `ccsdk/apps` is that `neng` and `vlantag-api` are missing the `vintage-engine` dependency and thus were not executing their `junit 4` tests and `sliboot` is running it's test but only has 5.
So long story short is that these insufficient or not running tests made it possible to update the parent to the spring boot 3 one, which broke all those modules.
[I started fixing it](https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/apps/+/141510) for `neng`, but due to the amount of changes, this is not something that we should rush today I would say.

So my suggestion is to revert the `neng` changes in this OOM CR. We can then run another gating which I expect to be green then.

inline_comments.message:
There are likely more changes needed for the `a1policymanagement` chart. [Looking at the logs from the gating run](https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/141289-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/k8s-teardown/pod-onap-a1policymanagement-0-a1policymanagement-logs.html), the service is not coming up due to a `database-enabled` property that is set in the `application.yaml` [in the repo](https://git.onap.org/ccsdk/oran/tree/a1-policy-management/config/application.yaml#n37). Perhaps this is being overwritten by the chart.

I think you'll have to update [the `application.yaml` in the chart](https://git.onap.org/oom/tree/kubernetes/a1policymanagement/resources/config/application.yaml) to fix it.
There also appear to be more values in there that should likely be defined. I think it's a good idea to basically copy over the one in the app repo to make sure it is in sync.

It would also be nice to make tracing configurable in a similar way like we do it in other places.
That is via a block in the `global`s of the chart to make it centrally configurable ([example here](https://git.onap.org/oom/tree/kubernetes/aai/values.yaml#n37)) and then [reference those values](https://git.onap.org/oom/tree/kubernetes/aai/components/aai-resources/resources/config/application.properties#n31) in the corresponding `application.yaml`.
The `management.tracing.enabled` is not currently defined for this service, but it would be great if we could add it.
Because it will lead to a ton of ugly log lines when tracing is on, but there is no jaeger deployed that could receive the traces.
That's why we have set `management.tracing.enabled` to `false` in all other places and made it globally configurable, such that it can be enabled with a single line of code when a jaeger is deployed.

I see way too many differences between the application.yaml in source and the one in the helm charts for my comfort.  Also, the version that I replaced (1.5.0) was quite old.  For now, I rolled back to that version and will ask the ORAN team to work a separate review to address the a1policymanagement microservice.
That sounds reasonable. I'll run another gating for patchset 2 and send you the results
